content.problemStatement:
A positive integer N‚Č•1N\ge 1N‚Č•1 is given.
An NNN-uple (x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xN)(x_1, x_2, \dots, x_N)(x1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč) of nonnegative integers is special if, after we remove all entries which are zero, its entries are nondecreasing. For example (0,2,0,0,4,0,4,5,0,0)(0, 2, 0, 0, 4, 0, 4, 5, 0, 0)(0,2,0,0,4,0,4,5,0,0) is special, but (3,2,0)(3, 2, 0)(3,2,0) and (0,1,3,0,0,1,0)(0, 1, 3, 0, 0, 1, 0)(0,1,3,0,0,1,0) are not.

One can show that there is a unique function őľ\muőľ defined on the NNN-uples of nonnegative integers such that, for any NNN-uple x=(x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xN)x=(x_1, x_2,\dots, x_N)x=(x1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč)

Output Format
